frameworks.name: signal-detection theory
frameworks.one_line: decomposes perceptual decision into target selection, evidence accumulation, and motor output stages

frameworks.name: drift-diffusion
frameworks.one_line: centro-parietal positivity treated as build-up of evidence accumulation toward bound

frameworks.name: neuromodulatory arousal
frameworks.one_line: tonic and phasic pupil diameter index distinct timescales of neuromodulator action

core_question: How do tonic and phasic pupil-linked arousal modulate distinct EEG stages of perceptual decision-making and behaviour?
key_assumptions: baseline pupil indexes tonic arousal; task-evoked pupil indexes phasic arousal
CPP reflects evidence accumulation; N2c/N2i reflect early target selection
neuromodulators act on multiple timescales to gate decision processing
manipulations: binning trials by baseline (tonic) pupil diameter
binning trials by task-evoked (phasic) pupil response
linear mixed-effects modelling of EEG signatures against RT and RT variability
key_findings: lower tonic and higher phasic arousal both shorten reaction times
tonic and phasic pupil predict dissociable sets of EEG signatures across decision stages
phasic arousal did not modulate motor build-up rate or N2c/N2i target-selection signals
CPP onset and build-up rate uniquely improved fit to behavioural RT and variability
interpretations: arousal-linked behavioural variability is mediated by attentional engagement, sensory processing, and decision-process variability rather than motor output
